worked: **Two-file split for the report** (REPORT.md punch-list + EXPERIMENT-NOTES.md meta) turned a "convoluted" deliverable into two focused docs serving distinct audiences. The user-explicit choice via `AskUserQuestion` got the right shape in one round instead of iterating.
**Per-doc imperative checkboxes** (`- [ ] Add X`) read as a TODO list — the user can pick off items without re-reading meta-context.
**Validator-driven cleanup**: running `docs-validator` after each round of edits gave a clear stop signal (0 MUST = stable baseline). Without it, "stable" would have been judgement-call territory.
**`AskUserQuestion` with `preview` options** worked well for both decision points (report shape, BTS handling) — user saw concrete examples and picked decisively.
**Investigating mechanical drift via `git status` + `rg` + Python link-resolver** caught structural drift quickly (10 broken links in `structure.md`).
didnt_work: **Sub-agent rendering markdown through TTY renderer** (`gum format`-style) — produced 2-space indented + 78-col hard-wrapped output with broken bold pairs. Saved as source = unusable. **Lesson:** when dispatching a sub-agent to produce a `.md` file, explicitly say "write raw markdown — no rendering, no piping through gum/bat/glow/mdcat" AND verify by reading raw bytes (`awk '{print length, $0}'`) before declaring done. Render-check beats trust.

**First REPORT.md was structurally OK but rhetorically wrong** — buried the lede, was 30% meta-experiment content, leaned on verdict-bingo instead of synthesis. Verifying file integrity (bold pairs, indentation) is NOT the same as verifying it serves the reader. **Lesson:** always read the artifact end-to-end with fresh eyes before claiming done.

**Sub-agent claimed writes were blocked** when re-dispatched for the two-file split. Almost certainly false (no such guard exists for general-purpose sub-agents with Write access). Sub-agent returned content inline; parent persisted it via Write. **Lesson:** when a sub-agent reports a "harness blocking write" issue, take the inline content and write it yourself — don't re-dispatch.

**My "3 gum-rendered \_index.md files need cleanup" finding was a false alarm** — the Read tool's display rendering of those files made them look 2-space-indented, but raw bytes are clean. Cost: ~5 mins investigating + a Bash spot-check to disprove. **Lesson:** when Read shows leading-space indents, verify with `awk 'NR<=10 {printf "[%d:%d] %s\n", NR, length($0), $0}' <path>` (raw bytes) before declaring the file broken.
gotchas: **Rate-limiting on parallel sub-agent dispatch** held throughout. Empirical sweet spot: 3 concurrent Agent calls (had been hitting limits at 4+). Used 3-at-a-time batches for Group C; all succeeded first try.

**Validator `_(planned)_` soft-broken-link convention is not yet supported** — the `by-role/_index.md`, `learning-paths/_index.md`, and `onboarding-router.md` all use `[text](path) _(planned)_` for forward-references. Validator counts them as broken. Worth wiring up.

**`docs/boring-technical-stuff/` partial migration** — restructure didn't drain BTS fully (`system/`, `testing/`, `vendors/`, `workflows/` moved into `tech/`; everything else remains in BTS). Documented in `structure.md` as "draining" per user's chosen handling.
notes: The persona trio (`greybeard`, `translator`, `pager-holder`) at `~/.claude/personas/` can be reused on any future doc batch via `Agent` tool dispatch. Convention: write reviews to `frontend/.claude/output/<date>-<slug>/reviews/` (mirror the 20260517-persona-review/ layout).

`frontend/.claude/output/20260517-persona-review/REPORT.md` is the canonical user-facing punch-list (139 lines, action-oriented). `EXPERIMENT-NOTES.md` (110 lines) holds the methodology + per-persona behavior + lens-divergence content.
`docs/structure.md` is now the canonical 1-page tour; the embedded INTERNAL maintenance comment lists update triggers + reconciliation order. Mirror that pattern when writing other meta-docs.
The `_(planned)_` link suffix is becoming a project convention for forward-references in scaffolds (3 docs use it now). Don't strip it; downstream tooling should learn it.

BTS is **draining, not deleted**. Don't auto-mass-move BTS files. The drain is opportunistic — moves happen when an author is touching the file anyway, OR when a sub-cluster has a clear new home. Forced wholesale migration risks breaking every cross-link in the tree.
feedback: "The report is so convoluted and makes no fucking sense" → drove the two-file split + per-doc imperative-checkbox punch-list pattern.

"Just get the docs to a somewhat-stable structure state, I'll do the rest" → confirmed scope discipline: fix MUST validator violations, don't touch persona-REPORT punch-list items or tooling improvements.

User explicitly chose: (a) two-file split for the report; (b) public onboarding-router (vs private checklist or per-hire template); (c) BTS draining (vs documenting dual reality vs skipping structure.md).
